mnemonics: PENDING Signals are Paused And PENDING delivery, like mail held at the post office.
keyPoints: The sigpending function retrieves the set of signals that are currently blocked from delivery and are pending for the calling process.
It is used to examine which signals have been raised (sent) but are currently being blocked by the process's signal mask.
The function takes a single argument, a pointer to a sigset_t variable, which it fills with the pending signal set.
commonMistakes: Assuming sigpending returns signals that have been delivered and handled (it only returns signals that are blocked and awaiting delivery).
Forgetting that the set returned is a snapshot; subsequent calls may return different results as new signals become pending or are delivered.
